violations.text: Salad mix was observed stored above the fill line of prep unit #2 and was held at 56 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food item was voluntarily disposed of during time of inspection. Proper storage methods discussed with PIC during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2025

Various food items were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2025

Working containers (spray bottles) used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Spray bottles were labeled during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2025

The cutting boards in the kitchen are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 07/26/2025

The floor and walls in the stove area has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2025

Shrimp, meatballs and salads packaged in the food establishment for self-service are lacking information required for labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, Allergens, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Establishment shall make necessary changes to labels. New Violation. Correct By: 07/20/2023

Wood is used in the construction of a panel on celling in Kitchen. Exposed Wood was also observed around hand sink at coffee station. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. Wood needs to be covered with a material that is nonabsorbent, smooth, durable and easily cleanable. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023
